name: Virtual Memory Paging
description: Paging translates a process's virtual address to a physical one: the address splits into a page number and offset, the TLB is consulted, and on a miss the page table is walked; if the page is not resident a page fault traps to the OS, which evicts a victim and loads the page from disk before forming the physical address.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Process virtual address"]
    N2["Split page# + offset"]
    N3["Lookup TLB"]
    N4{"TLB hit?"}
    N5["Walk page table"]
    N6{"Page in memory?"}
    N7["Page fault: trap OS"]
    N8["Evict victim page"]
    N9["Load page from disk"]
    N10["Update page table+TLB"]
    N11["Form physical address"]
    N12["Access memory word"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|yes| N11
    N4 -->|no| N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|yes| N10
    N6 -->|no| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 --> N11
    N11 --> N12

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N12 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Operating System Concepts (10th ed.)
sources.authors: Silberschatz, A.; Galvin, P. B.; Gagne, G.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.
